mechanisms.name: unattended-upgrades
mechanisms.risk: installs kernel images and REMOVES the running kernel unattended; the 2026-07-21 06:31 kernel swap (installed 7.0.0-28, removed running -22) fired from this mechanism ~2 minutes after the 06:29:49 timer run
mechanisms.observed_evidence: systemctl list-timers: apt-daily-upgrade.timer LAST 2026-07-21 06:29:49 — matching the incident timeline to the minute

mechanisms.name: apt-daily package-list refresh
mechanisms.risk: feeds the upgrade candidate set; harmless alone but arms the next unattended run

mechanisms.name: aptdaemon / update-manager interactive path
mechanisms.risk: applies desktop-breaking packages under a LIVE GNOME session; on 2026-07-21 at 19:44 it upgraded snapd 2.75->2.76, accountsservice, google-chrome, and vscode, destabilizing the running desktop (chrome crash 19:55, update-manager crash 20:02)

mechanisms.name: snap refresh
mechanisms.trigger: snapd internal timer (snapd.refresh not exposed as a systemd timer on this host)
mechanisms.schedule: snapd default 4x/day window; not readable unprivileged on this host (snap CLI absent from the agent PATH) — recorded as an observation boundary for the T9 observer
mechanisms.risk: snapd self-refresh restarts snap services mid-session (the 19:44 snapd upgrade rode the apt path, but snap self-refresh is an independent channel)

mechanisms.name: kernel install + reboot requirement
mechanisms.trigger: any of the above installing linux-image-*
mechanisms.schedule: follows the mechanism that carries it
mechanisms.risk: a kernel swap makes the eventual reboot mandatory and ends every live LifeOS process (shared-kernel boundary, spec section 3); survival depends entirely on the durable tier + re-attach (T7, now delivered)

desktop_breaking_packages.why: kernel swap; removes the running kernel and forces a reboot window
restarts snap infrastructure under live sessions (2026-07-21 19:44)
restarts the login/user service the desktop session depends on (2026-07-21 19:44)
in-place binary replacement crashes running instances (2026-07-21 19:55 chrome crash dump)
session-critical desktop components; upgrade mid-session risks the whole GNOME session

incident_reference.tie: the observed apt-daily-upgrade.timer LAST run (06:29:49) is the trigger of the 06:31 kernel swap; the 19:44 aptdaemon batch is the desktop destabilizer; both mechanisms above

governance_targets.observe: ARCHBP-110 — event observer over apt/dpkg logs and the timer surface (97 apt history entries readable unprivileged today)
governance_targets.hold_during_sessions: ARCHBP-111 — apt-mark hold / unattended-upgrades blacklist for kernel/snapd/accountsservice while a session is live (root, owner-gated)
governance_targets.deliberate_reboots: ARCHBP-112 — disable auto-reboot, require intent (root, owner-gated)
governance_targets.prove: ARCHBP-113/114/117 — host upgrade + drdave path with LifeOS unaffected
